title: ARA-290
description: ARA-290 (cibinetide) is a synthetic peptide derived from erythropoietin (EPO) that selectively activates the innate repair receptor. It promotes tissue repair and has neuroprotective properties without the blood-building effects of EPO.

research.title: Safety and Efficacy of ARA 290 in Sarcoidosis Patients with Symptoms of Small Fiber Neuropathy
research.summary: Investigated safety and effectiveness of ARA 290 in reducing small fiber neuropathy symptoms in 22 sarcoidosis patients, showing significant symptom improvement.

research.title: ARA 290, a Nonerythropoietic Peptide Engineered from Erythropoietin, Improves Metabolic Control and Neuropathic Symptoms in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes
research.summary: Found ARA 290 improved blood sugar levels, lipid profiles, and reduced neuropathic pain in type 2 diabetes patients.

research.title: Nonerythropoietic Erythropoietin Mimetic Peptide ARA290 Ameliorates Chronic Stress-Induced Depression-Like Behavior and Inflammation in Mice
research.summary: Demonstrated ARA290 reduces depression-like behavior in stressed mice similar to fluoxetine via anti-inflammatory effects.

research.title: The erythropoietin analog ARA 290 for treatment of sarcoidosis-induced chronic neuropathic pain
research.summary: Reviews ARA 290 as promising treatment for sarcoidosis-induced debilitating neuropathic pain.

research.title: ARA 290, a Peptide Derived from the Tertiary Structure of Erythropoietin, Produces Long-Term Relief of Neuropathic Pain
research.summary: Explored ARA290 effects on neuropathic pain in rats, finding dose-dependent pain reduction by decreasing inflammatory cell activity.

research.title: ARA290, a non-erythropoietic EPO derivative, attenuates renal ischemia/reperfusion injury
research.summary: Found ARA290 improved kidney function and reduced tissue damage in pigs after induced kidney injury.

sentiment.summary: Limited user reports, but those with neuropathy report significant symptom improvement. More commonly used in clinical settings than by individual biohackers.

effects.label: Clinical Evidence:
effects.description: Strong clinical trial data for neuropathy conditions.

effects.label: Targeted Action:
effects.description: Works specifically on tissue repair pathways.

dosage.label: Clinical Dosing:
dosage.description: 4mg subcutaneous injection, typically 3 times per week.

dosage.label: Research Use:
dosage.description: Dosing may vary in research contexts.

sideEffects.label: Generally Well Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Few adverse effects reported in clinical trials.

sideEffects.label: Injection Site Reactions:
sideEffects.description: Minor irritation at injection site possible.

availability.label: Research Chemical:
availability.description: Primarily available through research chemical suppliers.

availability.label: Clinical Trials:
availability.description: Has been used in formal clinical trials.

availability.label: Specialized Sourcing:
availability.description: Not as widely available as other peptides.
